topic.what: Patch-agnostic twofold-stealthy backdoor attack on Vision Transformers enabling backdoor activation across arbitrary patches
topic.how: Multi-location trigger insertion strategy (MIS) combined with bi-level optimization and adaptive backdoor learning framework to jointly optimize trigger and model parameters
topic.why_matters: Existing ViT backdoor attacks rely on fixed trigger insertion location making them detectable by defenses; PASTA enables backdoor activation in any patch while maintaining visual and attention stealthiness to evade both human and machine inspection

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of successful backdoor activations averaged across arbitrary patches
characteristics.evidence: Average attack success rate across all patches on 4 datasets
characteristics.context: Under the new patch-agnostic payload where trigger can be placed in any patch

characteristics.what_it_measures: Imperceptibility of trigger in pixel space measured by reconstruction error or perturbation norm
characteristics.evidence: L2-norm comparison with baseline stealthy triggers
characteristics.context: Visual stealthiness in pixel domain compared to prior attacks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Similarity of attention maps between clean and poisoned samples
characteristics.evidence: Attention map disparity minimization in optimization
characteristics.context: Attention imperceptibility in attention space compared to prior attacks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack effectiveness under state-of-the-art ViT-specific defenses
characteristics.evidence: Attack success rate under 3 ViT-specific defenses
characteristics.context: Robustness against backdoor defenses under patch-agnostic payload

characteristics.what_itasures: Average attack effectiveness when trigger is placed in neighboring patches vs training location
characteristics.evidence: TRE quantification across patches demonstrates inter-patch synergy
characteristics.context: Novel phenomenon discovered showing stronger TRE with inter-patch insertion

requires: Sufficient computational resources for bi-level optimization
Access to clean training data for poisoning

novelty: First observation of Trigger Radiating Effect (TRE) in ViTs where patch-wise triggers activate backdoor in neighboring patches
Patch-agnostic backdoor payload enabling backdoor activation across arbitrary patches during inference
Multi-location trigger insertion strategy (MIS) synergistically enhancing TRE through inter-patch training
Twofold stealthiness formulation covering both visual and attention imperceptibility
Bi-level optimization with adaptive backdoor learning framework for joint trigger and model optimization
open_problems: Extension to CNN architectures where TRE does not exhibit
Scalability to larger ViT models and datasets
Defense mechanisms specifically targeting patch-agnostic backdoor attacks
Investigation of other architectural inductive biases affecting backdoor effectiveness
